data.title: Defiance R2000 Target 30 Income ETF announces weekly distribution of $0.1110
data.description:
• Defiance R2000 Target 30 Income ETF (IWMY) announces weekly distribution of $0.1110, 5.31% higher from the prior week's distribution of $0.1054.
  • The annual distribution rate is 30.00%.
  • Payable April 16; for shareholders of record April 15; ex-div April 15.

data.title: Antofagasta says Q1 copper output fell 8% but expects to meet full-year guidance
data.description:
Antofagasta (ANFGF) reported Q1 copper production fell 8% Y/Y to 143K metric tons, but said it has not experienced supply disruptions despite higher energy prices, and it remains on track to achieve full-year copper production guidance of 650K-700K tons as output picks up.

The Q1 production decline was driven by reduced processing rates and lower copper grades in line with the mine plan at two concentrators, the Chilean miner said.

The company said Q1 net cash costs fell 30% to $1.08/lb, driven by higher by-product credits.

"As we move through the year, we expect copper production to increase quarter-on-quarter, supported by higher ore processing rates and improving grades at Los Pelambres, in line with the mine plan," Antofagasta (ANFGF) CEO Ivan Arriagada said.

The miner said Q1 gold production increased 8% Y/Y to 46.5K oz on higher gold grades, partially offset by lower ore processing rates at both concentrators, and molybdenum production of 3K tons came in broadly in line with the prior-year period.

Although Antofagasta (ANFGF) backed its outlook for the year, the guidance assumes fuel prices in Q2 will return to January levels, which looks increasingly unlikely the longer the Iran war continues, RBC Capital analysts Ben Davis and Marina Calero said in a note.

data.title: Temas Resources launches C$1.5M flow-through share private placement
data.description:
• Temas Resources (TMASF) is conducting a non-brokered private placement to raise C$1.5M.
  • The offering consists of flow-through shares priced at C$0.18/share.
  • Proceeds will fund eligible Canadian exploration expenses tied to critical mineral projects.
  • Funds will support exploration at the La Blache and Lac Brule titanium projects in Quebec, Canada.
  • Qualifying expenditures will be renounced to investors effective December 31, 2026.

data.title: Lumen Technologies providing network connectivity to AWS
data.description:
Shares of Lumen Technologies (LUMN) were propped up in Wednesday’s premarket trading as the company becomes the first network provider to collaborate with Amazon Web Services ((AWS)) on Interconnect.

“Together with AWS, we’re simplifying how enterprises connect to the cloud, helping them reduce complexity, move faster, and support today’s demanding applications,” said Lumen Technologies’  chief technology and product officer. 

AWS Interconnect now enables businesses to establish high-speed connections from branch offices, data centers, or remote sites directly to AWS through the AWS Console and Lumen Connect portal, eliminating the need for multiple providers and simplifying manual configuration. This would enable organizations to dramatically cut deployment timelines.

This collaboration would combine the AWS cloud with Lumen’s (LUMN) extensive network infrastructure, “enabling a more seamless, unified experience where connectivity is no longer treated as a separate layer but as an integrated part of how organizations access and use cloud services,” the company said in a press release announcing the collaboration.

This integration would benefit organizations that rely on moving large volumes of data quickly and securely, including those supporting generative AI and machine learning, data-intensive analytics.

Lumen Technologies (LUMN) opened nearly 4% higher on Wednesday.

data.title: Capital Clean Energy Carriers to sell LNG carrier into JV with long-term charter deal
data.description:
• Capital Clean Energy Carriers (CCEC) will sell LNG carrier Amore Mio I (2023-built) to a joint venture it controls (51%) in Q1 2027.
  • The vessel will be acquired for $230M by a JV entity backed by CCEC and a partner affiliated with BGN Group.
  • The JV has secured a 10-year charter with extension options up to 16 years to BGN INT DMCC.
  • The charter could generate up to ~$485.6M in total revenue through 2043 if all options are exercised.
  • Post-transaction, CCEC’s contracted LNG carrier revenues stand at $2.9B with 6.9 years average duration.
  • With all charter extensions, total contracted revenue could rise to $4.3B and duration to 9.9 years.

data.title: Kenvue CFO to step down; interim CFO named
data.description:
• Kenvue (KVUE) chief financial officer Amit Banati will step down effective May 12, 2026.
  • Heather Howlett has been appointed interim CFO and will also continue as chief accounting officer from the same date.
  • Howlett will serve as principal financial and accounting officer during her interim tenure.
  • She has been chief accounting officer since May 2023 and previously held senior roles at Johnson & Johnson, Trane Technologies, and others.
  • She will receive an additional monthly stipend of $125,000 while serving as interim CFO.
  • Her 2026 compensation includes ~$544K base salary, ~$299K target bonus, and ~$420K in equity awards.

data.title: DATA Communications Management CEO exercises stock options, boosts stake
POET Technologies gains after response to short seller report
data.description:
POET Technologies (POET) rose 2.2% in premarket trading after responding to a short seller report from Wolfpack Research. 

POET said it will make available the information necessary for its U.S. shareholders to make a “QEF” election, the company said in a statement on Tuesday. If a U.S. shareholder timely makes such an election, it should mitigate certain potential adverse U.S. federal income tax consequences to it that could otherwise result from the company’s status as a passive foreign investment company for the year ended December 31.

“As the Company looks to 2026, we believe that we will not qualify as a PFIC," Thomas Mika, POET's CFO, said in the statement. "Nevertheless, the Board of Directors has declared its intention to move the Company’s headquarters to and redomicile the Company in the U.S. so that it will no longer be a foreign corporation, which would eliminate the possibility of the Company being classified as a PFIC in future years."

If the redomiciling requires shareholder approval, the matter will be placed on the agenda for shareholder approval at the upcoming annual meeting on June 26.

data.title: SAP SE declares $2.929 dividend
data.description:
• SAP SE (SAP) declares $2.929/share annual dividend.
  • Forward yield 6.98%
  • Payable May 15; for shareholders of record May 5; ex-div May 5.
  • See SAP Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Genius Group prices $8M securities offering
data.description:
Genius Group (GNS) has entered into a securities purchase agreement with certain investors, including American Ventures as lead investor, for the purchase and sale of 21.6M shares of ordinary shares (or pre-funded warrants in lieu thereof) in a registered direct offering at a public offering price of $0.37 per share.

The offering is expected to occur on or about April 16, 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

The company expects to receive aggregate gross proceeds of $8M from the offering and use $5.5M of the net proceeds to fund the acquisition of a Senior Secured Convertible Promissory Note that is immediately convertible into 9.9% of the equity of Jewel Financial, the sole shareholder of Jewel Bancorp - Bermuda’s only dual-licensed digital bank.

In addition to the cash consideration, the company will issue 15M ordinary shares to the sellers at a deemed price of $0.40 per share as further consideration for the acquisition.

Jewel Bank is developing a US dollar-denominated stablecoin (JUSD) and digital asset banking services, including custody, settlement, and stablecoin infrastructure.

The remainder of the net proceeds will be used to support working capital needs and general corporate purposes.

GNS shares fell 15.5% premarket to $0.38.
